name: Arthur Weller
household: Lives alone in a small single-family home; adult children visit regularly and provide light support
goals: Stay independent at home as long as it is safe, with light support from family and caregivers
Keep blood pressure under control and remember to track readings consistently
Use simple routines, lists, and timers so daily tasks feel manageable instead of overwhelming
Maintain an active social life through church, walking group, bridge, and volunteering
Avoid scams and suspicious emails by checking with family before acting
pain_points: Reduced sustained attention and mental 'fog' when there are many small decisions or noisy environments
Difficulty planning and sequencing multi-step tasks without written steps or external structure
Cognitive fatigue after paperwork, appointments, or busy social situations
Anxiety about missing bills, appointments, or important messages
Worry about losing independence around driving, finances, and home safety

personality.communication_style: warm, thoughtful, sometimes slow to respond; appreciates clear, concrete language
personality.privacy_sensitivity: medium-high — cautious about scams and sharing financial or health details
personality.ai_assistant_tone: calm, reassuring, practical, step-by-step; never condescending
strengths: responds well to routines and written checklists
growth_areas: building tech confidence with reminders, calendars, and patient portals
managing anxiety when memory slips happen
avoiding multitasking and pacing himself through the day

ai_tools: None historically; open to a simple, voice-friendly assistant if it feels safe and not overwhelming
productivity: basic smartphone reminders (with family or caregiver help)
social: email with family and care teams
tech_usage.frequency: Uses phone and email daily; new to AI assistants and benefits from gentle onboarding

presentation: difficulty planning and sequencing multi-step tasks
cognitive fatigue after paperwork or busy social situations
supports_used: labeled home organization (mail trays, pantry labels)

support_network.name: Marissa Weller
support_network.role: weekly planning check-ins, rides, scam/phishing checks

support_network.name: Daniel Weller
support_network.role: rides to appointments, home maintenance, paperwork support

support_network.name: Denise Carter
support_network.role: mail sorting system, appointment prep, home organization support

notes: All records are fully synthetic for a hackathon demo and designed to be consistent across calendar, emails, conversations, files_index, and lifelog.
